title: ISic003461. Epitaph of Gregorios
body:
Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/P44QBETH
1[ἐν]θάδε κῆτε ὡ ἐν μακαρίᾳ
2[μ]νήμῃ Γρηγώριος υἱὸς Πο-
3[---] του πρ(εσ)β(υτέρου) ζήσας ἔτη κβ´
4[τ(ελ)]ευτᾷ δὲ ἐν Κ(υρί)ῳ μηνὴ Ἰου(λίῳ)
5ἰνδ(ικτιῶνος) η´

Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/P44QBETH
1[..]ΘΑΔΕΚΗΤΕΩΕΝΜΑΚΑΡΙΑ
2[.]ΝΗΜΗΓΡΗΓΩΡΙΟΣΥΙΟΣΠΟ
3[---]ΤΟΥΠΡΒΖΗΣΑΣΕΤΗΚΒ
4[.]ΕΥΤΑΔΕΕΝΚΩΜΗΝΗΙΟΥ
5ΙΝΔΗ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text after Rizzone 2009, checked against photograph;
  • 1: Manganaro restores a christogram at the start of the line, which can only be hypothetical
  • 3: Rizzone: Πο-|[θή]του; Manganaro πρ(εσ)β(ύτερος)
  • 4: Manganaro: ἐν κώ(μη)ͅ Μηνηνίου; ἰου(λίου) or ἰου(νίου) is possible.

translation

commentary

There seems no clear basis on which to decide whether Gregorios or his father was the presbuteros, since the ending is lost in the abbreviation, but it clearly affects the debate regarding celibacy of priests. Manganaro assumed the word referred to Gregorios; Rizzone to his father Po[--]tos.

bibliography

  • 2004.0665 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001283
  • 56.1107 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000796
  • 54.0944 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000796
  • 2006.0566 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001282
  • Manganaro 2005 35-37 fig.1 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001799
  • Manganaro Perrone 2006 92 n.20 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001787
  • Rizzone 2009 431 fig.2 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001634

lunate epsilon and sigma, uncial omega, delta with extended hasta, use of supralines.

Line 1 Interlineation line 1 to 2
